info.title: Marketstack API v2
info.description:
The official Marketstack API documentation will help you learn how to query the Marketstack JSON API for real-time, intraday, and historical stock market data, along with more advanced EDGAAR data endpoints. Also, define multiple stock symbols, retrieve extensive data about 2700+ Stock Exchanges Info, 30000+ Stock tickers from more than 50 countries, as well as 750 + Stock Market indexes, information about timezones, currencies, and more.

Our API is built upon a RESTful and easy-to-understand request and response structure. API requests are always sent using a simple API request URL with a series of required and optional HTTPS GET parameters, and API responses are provided in lightweight JSON format.

get.summary: Retrieve end-of-day stock price data for one or multiple ticker symbols across global exchanges.
get.description: Access end-of-day stock market data for single or multiple tickers using the `symbols` parameter.Supports over 500,000 tickers across 30+ global exchanges including NASDAQ, NYSE, and BATS.Daily-updated ticker lists are available and ticker symbols with periods should be replaced with hyphens (e.g. BRK.B → BRK-B).Prices may include adjusted values accounting for corporate actions such as splits and dividends using CRSP methodology.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve the latest available end-of-day market data for one or more ticker symbols.
get.description:
Returns the most recent end-of-day pricing data for the requested stock ticker symbols.
Use the `symbols` parameter to query one or multiple comma-separated tickers.
Supports numerous global exchanges and provides data including open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted values.
Useful for dashboards, real-time reporting, and last-close reference calculations.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve end-of-day market data for a specific date
get.description:
Returns historical end-of-day pricing data for a specific date using the `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
Optionally request exact timestamps in ISO-8601 format (e.g. 2020-05-21T00:00:00+0000).
Supports querying multiple ticker symbols using the `symbols` parameter.
Ideal for historical research, analytics, and backtesting.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Specific date for EOD data. Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

200.description: EOD data for the date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve intraday stock price data at minute-level intervals for supported US exchanges.
get.description:
Provides intraday market price data with intervals as short as one minute for US stock tickers on the IEX exchange.
Use the `symbols` parameter to request data for one or multiple tickers.
Ticker symbols containing periods must be replaced with hyphens (e.g. BRK.B → BRK-B).
Intraday data includes derived real-time reference pricing; bid/ask fields require IEX entitlement and will return null without appropriate authorization.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Intraday aggregation interval.

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

parameters.name: after_hours
parameters.description: If set to true, includes pre and post market data if available. By default is set to false.

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent intraday price bar for one or more ticker symbols.
get.description:
Returns the latest intraday bar for the requested stock ticker symbols.
Supports real-time updates, with sub-15-minute intervals available for Professional plan subscribers and above.
U.S. equity markets close at 4:00 PM EST; after-hours trades can be included using `after_hours=true`.
Intraday closing values may differ from official EOD auction-based closing prices.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Intraday aggregation interval.

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: after_hours
parameters.description: If set to true, includes pre and post market data if available. By default is set to false.

get.summary: Retrieve intraday price data for a specific historical date or timestamp.
get.description:
Returns historical intraday pricing data for the specified date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
You may provide an exact timestamp using ISO-8601 format (e.g. 2020-05-21T00:00:00+0000).
Supports one or multiple ticker symbols using the `symbols` parameter.
Useful for research, backtesting, and historical intraday analysis.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Specific date for intraday data. Example `2024-09-27 15:30`.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Intraday aggregation interval.

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

parameters.name: after_hours
parameters.description: If set to true, includes pre and post market data if available. By default is set to false.

200.description: Intraday data for the date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve real-time stock market prices across major global exchanges.
get.description:
Provides live intraday market pricing for stocks traded on major global exchanges.
Supports markets including NYSE, NASDAQ, LON, WSE, EPA, SHE, NSE, and more.
Ideal for real-time dashboards, monitoring, and trading workflows.
Returns continuously updated quote data for requested ticker symbols.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: Ticker for which to retrieve the stock price (e.g., `AAPL`).

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Optional exchange filter (e.g., `NASDAQ`).

get.summary: Retrieve real-time and historical price data for more than 70 global commodity products.
get.description:
Provides pricing data for 70+ widely traded commodities across energy, metals, industrial, agricultural, and livestock categories.
Includes real-time quotes, end-of-day pricing going back 15 years, and historical intraday data with up to 10,000 entries per interval.
Rate limited to 1 request per minute; refer to downloadable commodity list for supported items.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: commodity_name
parameters.description: Specify the commodity_name for which you like to receive the Price, aluminum for example.

get.summary: Retrieve historical commodity price data with configurable date ranges and frequency options.
get.description:
Provides historical pricing data for individual commodities with support for daily or monthly frequency using the `frequency` parameter.
Choose specific dates or ranges, with historical coverage extending up to 15 years depending on commodity type.
Recommended window is one-year data per request for consistency, with a rate limit of one call per minute.
Coverage varies across categories such as metals, energy, industrial, livestock, and agricultural, ranging from 9 to 15 years historically.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: commodity_name
parameters.description: Specify the commodity_name for which you like to receive the Price, aluminum for example.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.description: Specify the frequency of the commodity prices. Available options are `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`, `quarterly`, and `yearly`. Default is `daily`.

get.summary: Retrieve real-time analyst buy/sell/hold ratings with historical trend data and price targets.
get.description:
Provides real-time analyst recommendations including buy, sell, and hold, with updates as new reports are released.
Includes over 15 years of historical ratings and price targets for trend and performance analysis.
Ideal for investment research, market sentiment insights, and directional forecasting.
Rate limit enforced at 1 API call per minute.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: rated
parameters.description: Filter response on buy, sell, or hold.

get.summary: Retrieve stock split information and adjustment factors for supported ticker symbols.
get.description:
Provides stock split details including split ratios and adjustment factors for requested symbols.
Supports exchanges such as NASDAQ, PINK, SHG, NYSE, NYSE ARCA, OTCQB, and BATS.
Split information is also available for individual tickers using the Tickers endpoint.
Useful for pricing normalization, historical adjustments, and corporate action analysis.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve dividend payment information and history for supported ticker symbols.
get.description:
Provides dividend data including payment amounts and dates for requested stock symbols.
Supports major exchanges such as NASDAQ, PINK, SHG, NYSE, NYSE ARCA, OTCQB, and BATS.
Dividend information is also available for individual tickers via the Tickers endpoint.
Ideal for income investing, historical analysis, and yield tracking.

parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve detailed information and market data for a specific stock ticker symbol.
get.description:
Provides detailed information and exchange metadata for the requested ticker symbol.
Includes access to real-time, end-of-day, and intraday market data.
Useful for research, charting, analytics, and performance tracking.
Query using the {symbol} path parameter to look up an individual ticker.

get.description:
Returns end-of-day market pricing data for the specified ticker symbol, including open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted values.
Useful for charting, research, backtesting, and performance tracking.
Access by appending `/eod` to the ticker endpoint.

Includes open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted pricing values.
Useful for analysis, charting, and historical performance comparisons.
Use the {symbol} path parameter to request EOD data for an individual ticker.

get.description:
Returns dividend information for the specified ticker symbol including amounts, declaration dates, and ex-dividend schedules.
Useful for yield tracking, income investing analysis, and historical dividend research.
Ideal for dashboards and payout forecasting models.
Use the {symbol} path parameter to retrieve dividend data for an individual ticker.

get.description:
Provides live and historical intraday pricing data for the requested ticker symbol.
Supports minute-level intervals for US equities with real-time reference pricing.
Ideal for trading dashboards, short-term trend analysis, and market monitoring.
Use the {symbol} path parameter to retrieve intraday data for an individual ticker.

get.summary: Retrieve end-of-day pricing data for a specific ticker on a specified historical date or timestamp.
get.description:
Returns historical end-of-day price data for the specified ticker symbol using the `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
Supports exact timestamps in ISO-8601 format (e.g. 2020-05-21T00:00:00+0000).
Includes open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted price values.
Ideal for historical analysis, backtesting, and performance comparisons.

parameters.name: exchange

parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

200.description: Tickers eod data for the given date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent end-of-day pricing data for a specific stock ticker.
get.description:
Returns the latest available end-of-day price data for the specified ticker symbol.
Includes open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted pricing values.
Ideal for dashboards, performance snapshots, and reference close analysis.
Use the {symbol} path parameter to retrieve the most recent EOD record.

parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbol

parameters.name: exchange

200.description: Tickers eod data for the latest date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent intraday price bar for a specific stock ticker.
get.description:
Returns the latest intraday pricing data for the specified ticker symbol in real time.
Includes the most recent price bar with open, high, low, close, volume, and interval timestamp.
Ideal for trading dashboards, real-time monitoring, and short-term performance tracking.
Use the {symbol} path parameter to retrieve the latest intraday record.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbol

parameters.name: exchange

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

200.description: Tickers eod data for the latest date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve a complete list of all supported stock ticker symbols.
get.description:
Returns the full list of available ticker symbols supported by the API.
Useful for validation, autocomplete, and building searchable ticker datasets.
Supports global exchanges and can populate selection lists and dashboards.
Ideal for discovery and symbol lookup workflows.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter tickers by name or symbol.

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve detailed profile and metadata information for a specific stock ticker.
get.description:
Returns comprehensive profile and metadata for a stock ticker including company details and exchange information.
Useful for research, financial dashboards, symbol discovery, and analytics.
Supports comparison and metadata enrichment use cases.
Provides a complete overview of the target ticker.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

get.summary: Retrieve real-time and historical stock market index data, including a full list of supported benchmarks.
get.description:
Provides real-time and historical pricing data for major global stock market indexes.
Returns the complete list of supported benchmarks and index identifiers.
Useful for market comparison, performance tracking, dashboards, and analytical models.
Supports institutional research and benchmarking workflows.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve detailed information and real-time data for a specific stock market index.
get.description:
Provides detailed profile and metadata for a selected stock market index along with real-time and historical pricing data.
Returns attributes such as index name, symbol, exchange, and performance metrics.
Useful for benchmarking, comparison analysis, research dashboards, and performance tracking.
Supports analysis of index composition, trends, and historical movement.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Benchmark/index identifier (e.g., `australia_all_ordinaries`).

get.summary: Retrieve general information about more than 2,700 global stock exchanges.
get.description:
Provides detailed information and metadata for over 2,700 stock exchanges worldwide.
Useful for research, exchange validation, and building selection or discovery interfaces.
Not all exchanges listed here are supported across every Marketstack endpoint.
Refer to individual endpoint documentation to confirm exchange compatibility.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter tickers by name or symbol.

get.summary: Retrieve detailed information for a specific stock exchange using its MIC code.
get.description:
Returns detailed profile and metadata for a stock exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Includes attributes such as name, country, timezone, operational status, and supported symbols.
Useful for research, analytics, and verifying exchange coverage.
Use the {mic} path parameter to request exchange information.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

get.summary: Retrieve all ticker symbols listed on a specific stock exchange.
get.description:
Returns the list of ticker symbols traded on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Useful for symbol discovery, market screening tools, and exchange-focused analytics.
Supports global exchanges and provides visibility into available instruments.
Use the {mic} path parameter to request tickers for an individual exchange.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve end-of-day pricing data for all ticker symbols listed on a specific stock exchange.
get.description:
Returns end-of-day market data for every ticker traded on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Includes open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted price values where applicable.
Useful for bulk analysis, market dashboards, and exchange-level performance insights.
Use the {mic} path parameter to retrieve EOD data for all exchange-listed symbols.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: exchange
parameters.description: Filter your results based on a specific stock exchange by specifying the MIC identification of a stock exchange.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent end-of-day pricing data for all ticker symbols on a specific stock exchange.
get.description:
Returns the latest available end-of-day market data for every ticker traded on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Includes open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted pricing where available.
Useful for daily market snapshots, dashboards, and performance monitoring.
Use the {mic} path parameter to retrieve the most recent EOD dataset for all exchange-listed symbols.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

200.description: Latest EOD data for the exchange retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve end-of-day pricing data for all ticker symbols on a specific exchange for a selected historical date.
get.description:
Returns historical end-of-day market data for all tickers traded on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Specify the date using the `YYYY-MM-DD` format to access records for that day.
Includes open, high, low, close, volume, and adjusted pricing where applicable.
Ideal for historical analysis, comparison, and exchange-level performance evaluation.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Specific date for EOD data. Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

200.description: EOD data for the exchange and date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve intraday pricing data for all ticker symbols on a specific stock exchange.
get.description:
Provides real-time and intraday pricing data for every ticker symbol on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Supports minute-level intervals and real-time reference values.
Ideal for trading dashboards, short-term trend analysis, and exchange-level intraday monitoring.
Use the {mic} path parameter to request intraday data for all listed symbols.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Intraday aggregation interval.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use `ASC` for oldest first or `DESC` for newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent intraday pricing data for all ticker symbols on a specific stock exchange.
get.description:
Returns the latest available intraday price data for every ticker traded on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Includes interval values such as open, high, low, close, volume, and timestamp.
Useful for real-time dashboards, market monitoring, and exchange-wide performance analysis.
Use the {mic} path parameter to retrieve the most recent intraday dataset for all exchange-listed symbols.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Intraday aggregation interval.

200.description: Latest intraday data for the exchange retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve historical intraday price data for all ticker symbols on a specific exchange for a selected date.
get.description:
Returns historical intraday market data for all tickers traded on the exchange identified by its Market Identifier Code (MIC).
Specify the date using the `YYYY-MM-DD` format to access interval records from that day.
Includes interval pricing such as open, high, low, close, volume, and timestamps.
Ideal for backtesting, research, and exchange-level intraday performance analytics.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: One or more comma-separated ticker symbols (e.g., `AAPL,MSFT`).

parameters.name: mic
parameters.description: Exchange MIC identifier (e.g., `XNAS`).

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Specific date for intraday data. Example `2024-09-27 15:30`.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Intraday aggregation interval.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

200.description: Exchange intraday data for the date retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve a paginated list of all supported currencies available through the API.
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of supported currencies including identifiers, symbols, names, and related metadata.
Useful for validation, selection inputs, conversion tools, and currency lookup workflows.
Supports pagination using the `limit` and `offset` query parameters.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve a paginated list of all supported timezones available through the API.
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of supported timezones, including identifiers, offsets, and regional metadata.
Useful for timestamp conversion, scheduling, and aligning financial data by market hours.
Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset` query parameters.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve a full list of supported bond markets and associated country coverage.
get.description:
Returns the complete list of supported countries for which bond market data is available.
Useful for coverage validation, discovery workflows, portfolio allocation, and research.
Provides regional references that can be used to query additional bond endpoints.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve real-time government bond information for a selected country.
get.description:
Provides real-time government bond data focused on 10-year treasury notes issued by major countries worldwide.
Returns yield values, identifiers, and country-level metadata.
Useful for fixed-income research, macroeconomic analysis, and tracking interest rate movements.
Query by country to retrieve up-to-date treasury benchmark information.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name for bonds endpoint (e.g., `kenya`, `united%20states`).

get.summary: Retrieve a complete list of supported ETF tickers and holdings coverage information.
get.description:
Returns a full list of exchange-traded funds supported by the API, including identifier codes and ticker symbols.
Useful for ETF discovery, screening tools, and holdings analysis workflows.
Supports access to detailed ETF holdings when used with specific ETF endpoints.
Note: ETF API requests apply a call count multiplier of 20.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: list
parameters.description: ETF list type; currently `ticker`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve detailed ETF holdings data for a specified ticker and date range.
get.description:
Provides ETF holdings details filtered by date range using the `ticker`, `date_from`, and `date_to` parameters.
Returns composition and allocation data including asset symbols, weights, and quantities.
Useful for exposure analysis, portfolio research, and ETF composition tracking.
Note: ETF API endpoints apply a call count multiplier of 20.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: ETF ticker symbol.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter results from this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter results up to this date (inclusive). Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.

get.summary: Search and retrieve SEC CIK codes by full or partial company name.
get.description:
Returns CIK identifiers for companies matching a given name or partial name string.
Supports flexible searching using keywords, abbreviations, or full legal entity names.
Useful for EDGAR research, filing access, and regulatory compliance workflows.
Returns multiple matching records when the search term applies to multiple companies.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: company_name
parameters.description: Company name (min 3 letters) used to search CIK codes.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit (results per page). Default 100, maximum 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (number of results to skip). Default 0.

get.summary: Retrieve the official company name by providing a valid 10-digit SEC CIK code.
get.description:
Returns the registered company name for the specified 10-digit CIK code.
Requires the full CIK format including leading zeros—invalid formats will return an error.
Useful for compliance workflows, regulatory research, and linking EDGAR filings.
Returns data only when an exact match exists for the provided CIK.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: cik_code
parameters.description: 10-digit SEC Central Index Key (with leading zeros).

get.summary: Retrieve SEC EDGAR submission filings for a company using its CIK code or name.
get.description:
Returns detailed EDGAR submission records for the specified company using `cik_code` or `cik_code_name`.
Includes filing history, form types, dates, and submission metadata.
Useful for compliance research, financial disclosure tracking, and audit workflows.
Requires at least one identifying parameter.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: cik_code
parameters.description: The exact 10-digit CIK code including leading zeros (e.g., `0001509697`).

parameters.name: cik_code_name
parameters.description: The exact 10-digit CIK code including leading zeros ending with -submissions.json (e.g., `CIK0001509697-submissions.json`).

parameters.name: report_from
parameters.description: Filter filings by report date from this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: report_to
parameters.description: Filter filings by report date to this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: filing_from
parameters.description: Filter filings by filing date from this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: filing_to
parameters.description: Filter filings by filing date to this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: accession_number
parameters.description: Filter filings by exact accession number (e.g., `0001193125-20-209580`).

get.summary: Retrieve comprehensive financial and reporting facts for a company identified by its CIK.
get.description:
Returns all available company concept and financial fact data for the specified SEC CIK.
Includes reported metrics, disclosures, units, and time periods as submitted to EDGAR.
Useful for financial analysis, modeling, due diligence, and research.
Requires a valid CIK to retrieve structured fact records.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: cik_code
parameters.description: 10-digit SEC Central Index Key (with leading zeros).

get.summary: Retrieve all EDGAR disclosures for the Accounts Payable US-GAAP concept for a specific company.
get.description:
Returns financial disclosure facts for the US GAAP Accounts Payable concept for a company identified by its CIK.
Includes all facts grouped by reporting units such as USD or other currency equivalents.
Useful for historical analysis, reporting comparison, and financial statement research.
Aggregates concept-level disclosures into a single structured JSON response.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: cik_code
parameters.description: 10-digit SEC Central Index Key (with leading zeros).

get.summary: Company Concepts for US GAAP Accounts Payable in a Specific Unit
get.description: The frames API aggregates one fact for each reporting entity that is the last filed that most closely fits the calendrical period requested. This API supports annual, quarterly, and instantaneous data. This API endpoint is made for the US-GAAP taxonomy and the Accounts Payable Current type of the taxonomy.Where the units of measure specified in the API contain a numerator and a denominator, these are separated by “-per-” such as “USD-per-shares”. Note that the default unit in XBRL is “pure”.The period format is CY#### for annual data (duration 365 days +/- 30 days), CY####Q# for quarterly data (duration 91 days +/- 30 days), and CY####Q#I for instantaneous data. Because company financial calendars can start and end on any month or day and even change in length from quarter to quarter according to the day of the week, the frame data is assembled by the dates that best align with a calendar quarter or year. Data users should be mindful of the different reporting start and end dates for facts contained in a frame.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Marketstack API access key.

parameters.name: frame
parameters.description: This is the information of the frame for which we want to receive information.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The unit in which the data is requested. This parameter usually is USD. Validate and sanitize parameter inputs to ensure API security and expected input values.

200.description: Accounts Payable data in the specified unit retrieved.
